pages.body: This cinematic anthem transforms Cairo's struggles into a rhythmic celebration where music becomes defiance. Blending deep sub-bass with traditional oud and ney flute, the track builds from minor verses to an uplifting chorus of collective power. Smooth male and female duets weave through group chants in Egyptian Arabic, capturing modern nightlife energy without sentimentality. From rooftop views to sea breezes, every bar celebrates resilience and unity against fear. As darkness looms, rhythm grounds us; as doubt rises, a brighter sound emerges. This is more than pop—it is a proud declaration that love never breaks, lifting hearts when they feel like falling.
pages.styleSentence: A polished, radio-ready Egyptian pop R&B track at 104 BPM that fuses deep 808 sub bass and crisp club drums with traditional instrumentation like oud, qanun, darbuka, and ney flute to create a dark yet glossy soundscape; the arrangement features smooth male/female vocal duets layered with rhythmic group chants in Egyptian Arabic (heyya, yalla) over minor key verses that build into an uplifting chorus, capturing modern Cairo nightlife energy without cheesy sentimentality.

pages.text:
[Chorus - Female Lead, Immediate, No Intro]
We still dance when the night gets heavy
Still sing when the sky turns gray
Hands up if your heart stays ready
Love don’t break, we find a way

[Group Chant - Rhythmic, Egyptian Style]
Heyya, heyya
Yalla, yalla
Sawa, sawa
We rise, we stay

[Male Lead - Smooth R&B]
They only see the sand in the story
They only see the stone in the frame
But we got city lights, street drums, late nights
Millions of hearts with fire in the name

From Cairo rooftops to the river
From Alexandria out to the sea
We don’t need the world to understand us
We just need the world to breathe

[Female Lead]
Mama said keep your soul protected
Baba said keep your head up high
Even when the news gets reckless
Don’t let the fear move into your eyes

[Pre-Chorus - Male and Female]
And if the whole world shakes
We put rhythm in the ground
If the dark gets loud
We make a brighter sound

[Chorus - Full, Bigger]
We still dance when the night gets heavy
Still sing when the sky turns gray
Hands up if your heart stays ready
Love don’t break, we find a way

We still move when the drums start calling
Still shine when the lights go low
If one heart feels like falling
We lift it up and let it know

[Group Chant - Bigger]
Heyya, heyya
Yalla, yalla
Sawa, sawa
We rise, we stay

Heyya, heyya
Yalla, yalla
Sawa, sawa
Love leads the way

[Verse 2 - Male Lead, Confident]
This ain’t a postcard, this is people
This ain’t a camel in a scene
This is bass in the alley, kids in the traffic
Dreams in the dust trying to stay clean

This is gold in the voice of the aunties
This is tea when the night runs deep
This is jokes when the pressure gets ugly
This is faith when it’s hard to sleep

[Female Lead - Emotional]
I see mothers holding families together
I see fathers praying under streetlight glow
I see young ones building something better
With a beat in the blood and a long way to go

[Pre-Chorus - Call and Response]
When the world says break
[Group] We don’t break
When the fear says bow
[Group] We don’t bow
When the pain gets loud
[Group] We sing now
We sing now, we sing now

[Chorus - Full Club Drop]
We still dance when the night gets heavy
Still sing when the sky turns gray
Hands up if your heart stays ready
Love don’t break, we find a way

We still move when the drums start calling
Still shine when the lights go low
If one heart feels like falling
We lift it up and let it know

[Post-Chorus - Chant Hook]
Ya Masr, ya Masr
Heartbeat in the drum
Ya Masr, ya Masr
Love for everyone

Heyya, heyya
Yalla, yalla
Sawa, sawa
We rise, we stay

[Bridge - Cinematic, Ney Flute and Qanun]
No flag should be a reason for sorrow
No child should be afraid of tomorrow
From every border, every prayer
Every voice in the air

We are more than what they show you
More than war on a screen
More than pain in the headlines
More than ancient history

[Male Spoken-Sung]
This is for the strong ones
The tired ones
The ones still smiling when life gets wild
This is not just pride
This is peace with a pulse
This is culture with arms open wide

[Female Vocal Rise]
So let the oud cry
Let the drum talk
Let the whole room move like the Nile walk
If your heart hurts
Bring it close
We don’t dance alone

[Final Chorus - Biggest, Stacked Vocals]
We still dance when the night gets heavy
Still sing when the sky turns gray
Hands up if your heart stays ready
Love don’t break, we find a way

We still move when the drums start calling
Still shine when the lights go low
If one heart feels like falling
We lift it up and let it know

[Final Chant - Crowd, Percussive]
Heyya, heyya
Yalla, yalla
Sawa, sawa
We rise, we stay

Heyya, heyya
Yalla, yalla
Sawa, sawa
Love leads the way

[Outro - Short, No Fade]
Ya Masr, ya Masr
Still dancing
Still strong

pages.vocalStyle: Smooth R&B lead vocals with rhythmic group chants and call-and-response crowd elements

members.name: LEAD VOX
members.bio: Keeps the floor shaking. Never misses a downbeat.

members.name: BACKING VOX
members.bio: Weaves harmony layers without losing focus.

members.name: DRUMS
members.bio: Sets the pulse steady through chaos.

members.name: PERCUSSION
members.bio: Adds traditional flair to modern beats.

members.name: BASS
members.bio: Lowers the energy with deep, warm tones.

members.name: SYNTH
members.bio: Paints atmospheric textures over minor keys.

members.name: TEXTURES
members.bio: Scatters cultural dust for cinematic depth.

levels.section: Bridge (Group Chant + Verse 2 elements in transcript context)
levels.name: BRIDGE (GROUP CHANT + VERSE 2 ELEMENTS IN TRANSCRIPT CONTEXT)
